title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific markets are operationalizing new compliance frameworks this quarter, with Australia moving to active Travel Rule enforcement, South Korea developing institutional custody standards, and the Philippines implementing comprehensive VASP supervision. The region splits between mature licensing regimes (Hong Kong, Thailand) expanding product categories and emerging markets (Indonesia, Philippines) establishing baseline AML infrastructure. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: AMLA launches Q3 with direct supervision while the MiCA review (31 Aug deadline) determines DeFi and NFT treatment through 2027—France enforces its 1 July PSAN cutoff simultaneously. UK finalizes crypto authorization rules as Middle East markets (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Israel) operationalize licensing regimes Q3-Q4. 35 active country-level events create the densest compliance procurement cycle since MiCA passed.
